channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Fri, 08 May 2026 10:03:51 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: US, Iran weigh 30-day ceasefire plan, reopening Strait of Hormuz; n-talks still in limbo
item.description: Iran and the US are reportedly in talks for a temporary truce, aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and pave the way for broader nuclear negotiations. A 30-day pause in hostilities is proposed, with discussions focusing on lifting shipping blockades and ending military actions. More complex issues like sanctions relief and Iran's nuclear program remain under negotiation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: “You will be dead forever”: Sneako posts disturbing messages after Australia reportedly issues lifetime ban against streamer
item.description: Streamer Nico “Sneako” Kenn De Balinthazy shared another cryptic message on X saying, “I am ready to die,” after reports claimed Australia banned him from entering the country. Sneako denied being deported and later claimed federal agents followed him during his visit. His latest posts have gained major attention because of repeated death-themed messages shared over the past two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: "He just kissed me": IShowSpeed left horrified as chaotic livestream moment explodes across social media
item.description: A viral clip from IShowSpeed’s Dominican Republic livestream has sparked massive online discussion after a fan unexpectedly tried to kiss the streamer during a crowded bus interaction. Security quickly intervened, while the shocked creator repeatedly questioned what was happening on stream. The chaotic moment spread rapidly across Reddit and X, with many viewers defending Speed. The controversy grew further after xQc suggested the livestream’s reported 1.1 million viewers may have been inflated by bots.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Fact check: Was Candace Owens arrested on harassment charges after alleged Facebook threats?
item.description: Laura Loomer reignited her feud with Candace Owens by sharing a newspaper archive that allegedly showed Owens faced harassment and threatening charges in 2007. The report claimed Owens sent threatening Facebook messages, though no conviction records have surfaced. The clash escalated after Owens accused Loomer of doxxing and harassment. Loomer denied the allegations and called Owens a “pathological liar,” while warning that legal action could follow as their public social media battle intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: 'We’ll knock them out harder': Trump warns Iran of harsher military action after 3 US ships came under fire in Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump claimed three US destroyers were fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz but suffered no damage, stating Iranian attackers were destroyed. He issued a strong warning to Iran, threatening more violent military action if a deal is not signed quickly, calling the nation 'lunatics'.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why did Akaash Singh leave Flagrant? Andrew Schulz confirms heartbreaking podcast exit after nine years
item.description: Andrew Schulz announced Akaash Singh is leaving the Flagrant podcast after nearly a decade together. Singh is stepping away to fully focus on his growing stand-up comedy career and ongoing “Generational Triumph” tour after major performances, including a sold-out Radio City Music Hall show in New York.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: D4vd’s brother Caleb Burke breaks silence after TMZ claims and Celeste Hernandez backstage rumors explode
item.description: Caleb Burke denied rumors that he secretly brought Celeste Rivas Hernandez backstage at D4vd’s concert and criticized TMZ for falsely claiming he used the case to promote his music. Caleb clarified he was not close to D4vd for years, had already been making music long before the headlines, and only wants justice for Celeste.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Donald Trump criticised for politicising statement after death of legendary wrestling mogul Ted Turner
item.description: Donald Trump faced criticism after paying tribute to late media and wrestling mogul Ted Turner while simultaneously attacking CNN as “woke.” Critics argued the statement shifted focus from Turner’s groundbreaking legacy in broadcasting and professional wrestling to political grievances, overshadowing his transformative impact on CNN, WCW, and the Monday Night Wars.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: UFC sells $1.5 million White House tickets for ‘high rollers’ with access to McGregor’s 329 comeback fight
item.description: The UFC’s planned White House event has expanded into a multimillion-dollar spectacle, with $1.5 million VIP “Partner Investment” packages offering exclusive access alongside mass public ticket giveaways. Promoted as part of America’s 250th anniversary, the card blends sport, politics, and entertainment, featuring elite fighters while highlighting the growing partnership between the UFC, Dana White, and Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: US to revoke passports of parents over unpaid child support, starting with debts above $100,000
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US woman pleads guilty to $6.9 million Medicare fraud through false billing over 3 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hormuz technically open for ships, but risks too high, says Indian National Shipowners’ Association chief
item.dc:creator: Ismat Ara

item.title: Saahas Kaul and Neha Suratran: Indian-origin duo explain why people are speaking against India at Frisco city council meetings
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Nepal opposition wants PM Balendra Shah to hold Lipulekh talks with India
item.dc:creator: Vishant Agarwala

item.title: 'This is all about election': Immigration attorneys on Ken Paxton's investigation into 30 H-1B 'ghost offices'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bangladesh seeks China support for Teesta restoration project
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: Pak man spent 21 years in jail for murder in US, arrested again by ICE after release
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump invites UFC fighters to Oval Office and unveils never-seen-before Freedom 250 White House fight belt
item.description: Donald Trump has unveiled a custom championship belt and White House arena plans for UFC Freedom 250, scheduled for June 14 as part of America’s 250th anniversary celebrations. UFC stars Ilia Topuria, Justin Gaethje, Alex Pereira and Ciryl Gane joined Trump at the Oval Office ahead of the historic event, which will feature fights staged on the White House South Lawn.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: Arrest Near Sandringham Estate: Norfolk Police detain man; incident renews royal security concerns
item.description: A man was arrested near Prince Andrew's Sandringham residence on Wednesday evening for alleged intimidating behaviour and possessing an offensive weapon. The incident occurred in Wolferton, a village near the Royal Parkland, where the Duke of York is reportedly staying. Investigations are ongoing, with police having received reports of aggressive conduct.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Afghanistan’s ‘one-kidney village’ where residents sold organs to survive
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
